question_text: All electromagnetic waves travel at 3 × 10^8 m/s in a vacuum. If a radio wave has a frequency of 6 × 10^6 Hz, what is its wavelength? (Use: wave speed = frequency × wavelength)
explanation: Rearranging the equation: wavelength = wave speed ÷ frequency = (3 × 10^8) ÷ (6 × 10^6) = 50 m. This is a realistic radio wave wavelength and tests the ability to use the wave equation with standard form numbers.
